title: More than 1 million Philippine overseas workers at risk amid Middle East crisis
author: Johnny Wan
fullText: Video | More than 1 million Philippine overseas workers at risk amid Middle East crisisPhilippine government has no mass repatriation plan, leaving many workers to weigh risks against the livelihoods of their families back home02:23Philippine overseas workers under fire as Iran conflict worsensPhilippine overseas workers under fire as Iran conflict worsensPublished: 11:34pm, 3 Mar 2026Updated: 12:11am, 4 Mar 2026Philippine carers have described racing to bomb shelters in Israel as the US-Israeli war in Iran sends missiles across the Middle East.More than 1.1 million overseas workers from the Philippines are based in the region, with over 96 per cent concentrated in five countries closely tied to the fighting, including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait and Bahrain.One Philippine carer has already been killed during an air strike.0.8x0.9x1.0x1.1x1.2x1.5x1.75x
summary:
Philippine carers have described racing to bomb shelters in Israel as the US-Israeli war in Iran sends missiles across the Middle East.
More than 1.1 million overseas workers from the Philippines are based in the region, with over 96 per cent concentrated in five countries closely tied to the fighting, including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait and Bahrain.
One Philippine carer has already been killed during an air strike.
